name: Photovoltaic cell, multi-Si; 2018 consumption mix, at wholesale
description:
This cradle-to-gate unit process dataset represents the 2018 market mix of 1 m2 of multi-Si cells.
This unit process includes raw material acquisition and transport.
category: 42: Wholesale Trade/4236: Household Appliances and Electrical and Electronic Goods Merchant Wholesalers

processDocumentation.timeDescription: Frischknecht et al. (2020) indicates this is the production mix for the (calendar) year 2018.
processDocumentation.geographyDescription:
Frischknecht et al. (2020) included consumption mixes for the US and Europe. The Us consumption mix is made of US made cells, and of cells made in the Asia & Pacific region
IMPORTANT NOTE: Due to the purposes of the project in which this inventory was included in the Federal LCA Commons, and to the absence of Korean electricity, the material input to this market are US-made cells in both cases, not Korean.  Updates are planned to add international grids in to represent consumption mixes more accurately. Transport represents the US consumption mix distances.
processDocumentation.technologyDescription: This unit process is a market activity, representing the consumption mix of multi-Si cells, and an estimation on the distance the wafer is transported.
processDocumentation.modelingConstantsDescription: Frischknecht et al (2020) assumed a truck transport of 50 km and an additional 200 km train transport.
processDocumentation.completenessDescription:
Treatment of Missing Environmental Data
Frischknecht et al. (2020) offered no information on cutoff criteria or flows that might have not been considered. However, no direct emissions are expected in a market activity.
Treatment of Missing Technosphere Data 
Frischknecht et al. (2020) offered no information on cutoff criteria or flows that might have not been considered. However, the Technosphere flows present are consistent with an intercontinental market activity.
Mass Balance
The mass balance for this process was not calculated.
processDocumentation.dataSelectionDescription: The inventory present in Frischknecht et al. (2020) Table 18 used here their own estimations (see Modeling constants above). No source is given there for the market share.
processDocumentation.dataTreatmentDescription:
The values in the LCI in Frischknecht et al. (2020) reflect the expert judgement of the authors, since there is no suggestion of a weighted average from different sources/reports.
Process data quality
Based on the information included in the “Data completeness section”, the data quality characteristic (DQC) "Process completeness" was given a score of 1 (>80 % of determined flow have been evaluated and given a value). It is likely the original report went through a review process, but because it is not explicitly mentioned, the data quality characteristic "Process review" was left as default (No documented review) and thus with a score of 5.
processDocumentation.samplingDescription:
System boundary conditions
The system boundary is cradle to gate and includes although as a market activity, it only includes material use and transport. 
Data collection
The inventory present in Frischknecht et al. (2020) Table 18 seems to be based solely on the expert knowledge of the authors of that report.
Uncertainty
Frischknecht et al. (2020) included a standard deviation for all flows, which is repeated here.
processDocumentation.dataCollectionDescription: The transport was specifically estimated for Frischknecht et al. (2020) report, but although no sources are given, the inventory is said to represent production for the calendar year 2018, so data collection period must have taken place at some point after that.

reviews.details: It is likely the original report went through a review process, but because it is not explicitly mentioned, the data quality characteristic "Process review" was left as default (No documented review) and thus with a score of 5.

processDocumentation.intendedApplication:
Situation C2 - Accounting, without system-external interactions - The intended application is a purely descriptive accounting / documentation of the analyzed system excluding interactions with other systems in the LCI model.
The target audience of this model are LCA practitioners, the silicon, solar and related industries and the general public. The inventory is considered largely complete, except for land transformation activities. For that reason, it is not recommended to use this dataset when evaluating land use impact categories. The results of the National Institute of Standards and Technology’s data gap analysis were analyzed using the ISO 21930-LCIA-US impact assessment methodology, as well as Cumulative Energy Demand and a subset of ReCiPe 2016 impact categories.
The original application of this dataset was the identification of data gaps in the Federal LCA Commons (FLCAC) (see "Project" above). However, the intended application is to provide a cradle-to-gate inventory (A1-A3, according to ISO 21930) using exclusively processes currently available in USLCI or generated as part of the project mentioned above, and elementary flows from the Federal Elementary Flow List (FEDEFL) to enable the use of a fuller range of LCIA impact categories than those reported in EPDs. For data quality purposes, the intended application is to provide a US average inventory for the year of publication of the original source.
processDocumentation.projectDescription: This dataset was developed as part of the “Public Life Cycle Inventory Data Gap Analysis for Silicon-based Photovoltaic panels” National Institute of Standards and Technology’s Technical Note.
